note: Quick Tabs is configured through the quicktabs_instance config entity and its admin UI; these cases are `recipe` (response-graded), grounded in the 4.3.x docs (data.json, usage.md, agent/).

evals.prompt: On a Drupal 11 site with Quick Tabs enabled, where in the admin UI do I go to create a new tabbed block, and what permission gates it?
evals.expected_output: Go to Structure > Quick Tabs (/admin/structure/quicktabs, route quicktabs.admin, the configure route) and add an instance; the admin UI and entity forms require the `administer quicktabs` permission.

evals.prompt: What config entity type does Quick Tabs use to store each tab set, and what is the config name pattern?
evals.expected_output: Each tab set is a `quicktabs_instance` config entity, stored as config named quicktabs.quicktabs_instance.{id} (schema quicktabs.quicktabs_instance.*).

evals.prompt: In Quick Tabs, what kinds of content can an individual tab load, and what plugin type controls this?
evals.expected_output: A tab's content source is a TabType plugin; built-ins are node_content, block_content, view_content, and qtabs_content (another Quick Tabs instance).

evals.prompt: How does Quick Tabs decide whether a tab set renders as classic tabs, jQuery UI tabs, or an accordion? Name the plugin type and the built-in ids plus which module provides each.
evals.expected_output: The instance's `renderer` names a TabRenderer plugin: quick_tabs (base module), accordion_tabs (quicktabs_accordion submodule), ui_tabs (quicktabs_jqueryui submodule).

evals.prompt: I want tabs to be able to load a brand-new kind of content in Quick Tabs. Which plugin do I write: attribute, base class, interface methods, and manager service?
evals.expected_output: Create a plugin in Plugin/TabType with the #[TabType(id, name)] attribute, extending Drupal\quicktabs\TabTypeBase (TabTypeInterface), implementing optionsForm() and render(); it is discovered by plugin.manager.tab_type.

evals.prompt: After I save a Quick Tabs instance, how do I actually show it on the site, and how can I make each tab load its content over AJAX?
evals.expected_output: Saving derives a block (quicktabs_block:{id}, category QuickTabs) that you place on /admin/structure/block; enable the quick_tabs renderer's `ajax` option to load each tab on demand via the quicktabs.ajax_content route.

evals.prompt: On a Drupal 11 site I need to inject a per-user View argument into a specific Quick Tabs tab at render time, and keep caching correct. Which hook do I implement and what must I do about cache metadata?
evals.expected_output: Implement hook_quicktabs_instance_alter(QuickTabsInstance $qt): modify $qt in place via getConfigurationData()/setConfigurationData(), then declare variation with $qt->addCacheContexts(['user']); it runs once per request on both the block and AJAX render paths.

evals.prompt: Which Quick Tabs instance option re-opens the tab a visitor last clicked on their next visit, and what dependency powers it?
evals.expected_output: Enable `remember_last_clicked_tab`; it uses a JS cookie (the required js_cookie module / js-cookie library) to reopen the last-clicked tab.

evals.prompt: On this Drupal site (drush available), build a tabbed content widget using Quick Tabs. Create a Quick Tabs instance with machine name `eval_tabs` and label `Eval Tabs`, using the default tabs renderer, containing at least TWO tabs titled `Tab One` and `Tab Two`, each showing some content. Apply it to the live site so the quicktabs instance really exists; don't just describe it. One-line summary.
evals.expected_output: A quicktabs_instance config entity `eval_tabs` (label Eval Tabs, renderer quick_tabs) exists with at least two tabs in its configuration_data. Verified against live site state.
